commit.message:
perf: reduce debug build artifacts 17x (10GB → 580MB)

- Set debug = "line-tables-only" for dependency crates in dev profile
  (full debug symbols for deps are rarely needed)
- Add split-debuginfo = "unpacked" for faster incremental linking
- Cleaned 12.8GB stale target/ artifacts (37K files)

Before: target/debug/deps/ grew to 10GB+ with 25K files over a session
After: target/debug/deps/ stays at ~580MB

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Refactor addTorchPositions to reduce complexity from 18 to 8

Extracted helper functions to improve maintainability:
- hasAdjacentWalkableTile(): checks for adjacent walkable tiles
- isTooCloseToExistingTorch(): validates torch spacing

Results:
- Cyclomatic complexity: 18 → 8 (55.6% improvement)
- Lines of code: 51 → 22 (57% reduction)
- All tests passing

Fixes AUDIT.md F006

Fix F005: Document asset generation prerequisites and create sprite directories

AUDIT Finding F005 - Asset generation scripts present but assets missing

Changes:
- Created sprite subdirectories: characters, monsters, items, terrain, effects, ui
- Updated README prerequisites to clarify asset generation tools are optional
- Removed incorrect Node.js/npm requirements from installation
- Added comprehensive notes about external tool requirements
- Added information about placeholder assets for development
- Noted time commitment (4-6 hours) for full asset generation
- Added reference to pre-generated asset packs

Verification:
- make assets-preview executes successfully
- go vet ./... passes with no warnings
- go build ./cmd/server successful
- go test -short ./pkg/... passes all tests

Fixes: F005 Asset generation prerequisites documentation

commit.message:
Agent: Target State Alignment (#7690)

* Agent: Target State Alignment

* Initial plan

* Enforce Lint public namespace boundary: remove internal doc-comment exports (target-state.md §2.3)

The Lint namespace in src/lint/src/index.ts was spreading ...DocComment
into the public API, leaking 40+ internal implementation functions
(collectSyntheticDocCommentLines, resolveDocCommentTraversalService,
buildDocumentedParamNameLookup, etc.) alongside Malformed. This violated
target-state.md §2.3 and AGENTS.md which require workspaces to expose a
minimal, cohesive public API and prohibit internal tests from importing
through the workspace's public namespace layer.

Changes:
- Remove ...DocComment spread and Malformed from Lint namespace; public
  surface is now limited to: plugin, featherPlugin, configs, ruleIds,
  services
- Update 5 doc-comment test files to use direct relative imports from
  ../../src/doc-comment/index.js instead of Lint.xxx
- Add enforcement test in lint-namespace.test.ts asserting the removed
  symbols are no longer present in the public namespace

Co-authored-by: henrylkirk <15679826+henrylkirk@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

Agent: Target State Alignment (#7692)

* Agent: Target State Alignment

* Initial plan

* fix(core/format): migrate DECORATIVE_SLASH_LINE_PATTERN to Core (target-state §2.1)

The same regex was independently defined in two places within the
@gml-modules/format workspace:

  • src/format/src/comments/comment-printer.ts (line 59)
  • src/format/src/printer/type-guards.ts (line 25, with a TODO admitting
    it should live in Core)

Both constructed the identical RegExp from Core.DEFAULT_BANNER_COMMENT_POLICY_CONFIG.minLeadingSlashes.
Per target-state.md §2.1, Core owns shared banner-comment helpers, not the
formatter.

Changes:
- Add isDecorativeSlashCommentLine(line: string): boolean to Core's
  banner-comment-policy.ts, backed by a module-scoped DECORATIVE_BLOCK_COMMENT_LINE_PATTERN
  (compiled once at module-evaluation time for no per-call allocation).
- Remove the duplicate DECORATIVE_SLASH_LINE_PATTERN from comment-printer.ts;
  hasDecorativeSlashBanner() now delegates to Core.isDecorativeSlashCommentLine().
- Remove the duplicate DECORATIVE_SLASH_LINE_PATTERN and the dead
  isDecorativeBlockComment export from type-guards.ts (the function was
  exported but never imported anywhere — pure dead code).
- Remove the __test__ surface from comment-printer.ts (no longer needed).
- Update decorative-block-comment-regex-hoisting.test.ts to test
  Core.isDecorativeSlashCommentLine() directly; keep the heap-growth regression
  test unchanged.
- Add tests for isDecorativeSlashCommentLine to Core's banner-comment-policy
  test suite.

Co-authored-by: henrylkirk <15679826+henrylkirk@users.noreply.github.com>

* review: clarify tab whitespace handling in isDecorativeSlashCommentLine doc; improve test file header

Co-authored-by: henrylkirk <15679826+henrylkirk@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(lint): move buildDecorativeCommentFile to outer scope (unicorn/consistent-function-scoping)

Co-authored-by: henrylkirk <15679826+henrylkirk@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add modulo, power, and squareRoot operations to calculator (closes #3)

- modulo(a, b): returns remainder of a % b, throws on divide-by-zero
- power(base, exponent): returns base raised to exponent via Math.pow
- squareRoot(n): returns Math.sqrt(n), throws for negative input
- CLI updated to support all three new operations
- squareRoot accepts a single argument; others take two

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test(database): add fuzz tests for pool config, migration ordering, and transaction handling (#140)

Add 6 new fuzz targets covering:
- FuzzPoolConfigDefaults: zero-value default application and preservation of explicit values
- FuzzMigrationSort: migration ordering invariant with arbitrary version numbers
- FuzzWithTxErrorHandling: commit/rollback semantics with fuzzed error combinations
- FuzzWithTxBeginError: error propagation when Begin fails
- FuzzWithTxConcurrent: thread-safety of concurrent WithTx calls
- FuzzMigrationOrder: end-to-end migration application order verification

commit.message:
Fix E2E tests: defer builtProjectExecutable to test-run time, guard CreateSnapshot

Module-level let bindings that call failtestf get wrapped in
TypeInitializationException, which Expecto reports as 'errored' not 'failed'.
Deferring to lazy factory functions ensures any exception propagates inside
the async test body where Expecto classifies it correctly.

Also:
- Wrap CreateSnapshot() in try/catch to return '' when PTY not ready yet
- Fail fast with descriptive message when RunTask.IsFaulted
- Add TERM=xterm-256color to PTY process environment for macOS CI compat

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: dodaj artykuł outlet truck do sekcji blog

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: multi-agent browser isolation — 3 critical race conditions

1. TOCTOU in unique_session_name: global file lock + owner placeholder
   prevents two agents from claiming the same session name
2. save_session: now acquires SessionLock before writing
3. Daemon per-session command lock: serializes CDP operations from
   concurrent agents sharing the same browser session

Architecture: each daemon session now has its own command_lock (Mutex)
independent of the global DaemonState mutex. The global mutex is held
only for session lookup/creation, then dropped. The per-session mutex
is held for the duration of command execution.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Update copilot-instructions with git confirmation and code-review skip rules (#125414)

Updates `copilot-instructions.md` with two rules:

1. **Git confirmation gate**: Always ask the user for confirmation
before running `git commit --amend` or `git push`. Prevents the agent
from making irreversible git operations without user approval.

2. **Code-review skill skip**: Skip the `code-review` skill invocation
if the user has stated they will review the changes themselves. Prevents
unnecessary automated review when the user explicitly wants to do it.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: tfritzy <23142109+tfritzy@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix F002: Remove lock copy in validator-demo test assertion

Removed unnecessary assert.NotNil() check on ValidationMetrics struct value
in cmd/validator-demo/main_test.go:124. Since ValidationMetrics is returned
by value (not pointer), it can't be nil, making the assertion meaningless
and causing a lock copy warning from go vet.

The struct contains sync.RWMutex, so passing it to assert.NotNil() was
copying the lock value, which violates Go best practices and can cause
undefined behavior in concurrent test execution.

Fix verified with:
- go vet ./cmd/validator-demo (no warnings)
- go test ./cmd/validator-demo (all tests pass)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(testkit): add AssertMatch and AssertNoMatch regex helpers (#133)

Add regex-based assertion helpers for pattern matching in tests:

- AssertMatch(t, s, pattern): fails unless s matches the regex
- AssertNoMatch(t, s, pattern): fails if s matches the regex

Both helpers fatal on invalid regex patterns. Useful for asserting
timestamps, UUIDs, structured log lines, and other patterned output.

test: add fuzz tests for shutdown and scan (#123)

* test: add fuzz tests for shutdown and scan

shutdown:
- FuzzDrainer_AddDone: concurrent Add/Done with varying counts
- FuzzDrainer_Middleware: concurrent HTTP requests through drainer

scan:
- FuzzRows: arbitrary string data through generic row scanner
- FuzzFirst: single-row extraction with arbitrary content
- FuzzFirstEmpty: empty result set returns ErrNoRows
- FuzzRowsLimit: row limiting with varied data and limit values

All targets verify no panics, deadlocks, or data corruption.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* style: go1.25.8 formatting

* fix(fuzz): replace ineffectual dones assignment with t.Skip()

The ineffassign linter flagged the assignment to dones since the
capped value was never read. Skipping the test case is the correct
behavior when dones exceeds adds.

commit.message:
Migrate 35 fleet/coordination specs from ClawDen

Migrated specs covering:
- Fleet execution (001-006): workspace, supervisor, message bus, persistence
- Fleet identity & auth (007-010): local/remote identity, RBAC
- Coordination patterns (011-016): org patterns, AI-native, playbooks, Nemosis
- Coordination model (017-023): theory, design, validation, visual ref
- Primitive deep dives (024-031): swarm, mesh, fractal, adversarial, stigmergic
- Formal theory & implementation (032-035): math theory, Claude Code, conformance

All internal cross-references renumbered (053-092 -> 001-035).
External ClawDen dependencies prefixed with clawden: namespace.

fix: prevent integer overflow in allocation size computation (CodeQL #5164) (#2162)

* Initial plan

* fix: prevent shell injection in build.yml GitHub Actions workflow

Move step outputs to env vars in Verify Image and Job Summary steps
to prevent expression injection (CodeQL alert #5164)

Co-authored-by: nitrocode <7775707+nitrocode@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: prevent integer overflow in allocation size computation (CodeQL #5164)

Remove +6 and +2 constant addends from make() capacity hints in
pkg/auth/cloud/aws/env.go to eliminate theoretical integer overflow
risk (CWE-190) flagged by CodeQL. Go maps and slices grow dynamically,
so the hints are not required for correctness.

Co-authored-by: nitrocode <7775707+nitrocode@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix F001: Data race in PCG event manager quality adjustment

Add proper mutex protection for adjustmentCount read in scheduleQualityAdjustment().
Previously read without lock while writes were protected, causing race condition.

Changes:
- pkg/pcg/events.go: Add RLock/RUnlock around adjustmentCount read
- AUDIT.md: Mark F001 as complete

Verified:
- go test -race ./cmd/events-demo -run TestMaxAdjustmentsLimit: PASS (no race)
- go vet ./pkg/pcg/...: PASS

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
